title: Interpretation Metadata Schema (Versioned Pair Classification)
description: Canonical contract for `results/{pair_id}/interpretation_metadata.json` — the pair-level classification record read by Ace's landing-page loader, Ray's narrative generator, Evan's method routing (Rule C1), and Vera's chart encoding. Governed by DATA-D6 (producer rule) and META-CF (Contract File Standard). Closes two distinct bug classes: (1) the ad-hoc-field-addition pattern (Wave 3 expansion — indicator_nature, indicator_type, strategy_objective each added in separate waves with no versioned shape); (2) the multi-writer race condition (ECON-CFO-1 — three agents write to the same JSON with no ordering protocol), closed here by the `owner_writes` explicit mapping. Adding a new field to this taxonomy requires a schema bump, not a one-off ad-hoc field. Cross-references: DATA-D3 (classification decision procedure), DATA-DD4 (classification ownership), META-CFO (classification field ownership), GATE-19 / GATE-20 / GATE-21, META-IA (interpretation annotations), APP-LP5 / APP-LP6 (landing-card chips), RES-IT1 (Ray's strategy-objective ownership).

pair_id.description: Canonical pair identifier (snake_case, e.g. `hy_ig_v2_spy`). Matches the `results/{pair_id}/` directory and the landing-card key.

schema_version.description: Semver of the contract shape this instance conforms to (matches `x-version` of the governing schema file). DATA-D6: Ace's loader MUST check this field and MUST NOT defensively fall back for unknown versions — mismatch is a hard error until the loader is updated in the same commit as a schema bump.

indicator_nature.description: Dana-owned. Cycle timing of the indicator relative to the business/market cycle. Controlled vocabulary per DATA-D3 Step 2. Source of truth order: (1) Conference Board classification, (2) NBER/Fed research consensus, (3) published econometric tests. Never classify from intuition alone. GATE-19 blocker if missing/empty/`unknown`.

indicator_type.description: Dana-owned. Controlled vocabulary shared with Evan's Rule C1 category routing. Canonical 7 values from GATE-20 (`price`, `production`, `sentiment`, `rates`, `credit`, `volatility`, `macro`) plus two extensions from the data-series-catalog (`survey`, `housing`). Near-synonyms (`activity`, `output`, `vol`, `fx`) are REJECTED — map to canonical at source. GATE-20 blocker if missing/empty/`unknown`.

strategy_objective.description: Ray-owned (set after tournament results known, per RES-IT1 / DATA-DD4). `procyclical_capture` = long when indicator confirms bullish regime; `countercyclical_protection` = defensive when indicator signals stress; `regime_switching` = symmetric long/short across regimes; `tail_hedge` = asymmetric protection against extreme left-tail episodes; `min_mdd` / `max_sharpe` / `max_return` = legacy tournament-objective labels retained for HY-IG v2 and earlier pairs. GATE-21 blocker if missing/empty/`unknown`.

owner_writes.description: Explicit field-ownership mapping. Each listed agent is the SOLE writer for its listed fields. Closes ECON-CFO-1 (multi-writer race): the consolidated `interpretation_metadata.json` is produced by atomic merge of agent-specific partial JSONs in a deterministic order (dana → evan → ray), and any field not listed here CANNOT be added without a schema bump (DATA-D6). This forces new taxonomy extensions through registration rather than ad-hoc field drift.

dana.description: Fields Data Dana owns exclusively. Default set includes `indicator_nature`, `indicator_type`, `known_stress_episodes`, `data_provenance`. Schema-required fields assigned to Dana: `indicator_nature`, `indicator_type`.

evan.description: Fields Econ Evan owns exclusively. Default set includes `observed_direction`, `direction_consistent`, `key_finding`, `confidence`. Evan writes AFTER Dana, can read Dana's fields but never overwrites them.

ray.description: Fields Research Ray owns exclusively. Default set includes `strategy_objective`, `narrative_summary`, `expected_direction`, `mechanism`, `caveats`. Ray writes LAST (after tournament is complete), can read Dana's and Evan's fields but never overwrites them. Schema-required field assigned to Ray: `strategy_objective`.

last_updated_by.description: Identifier of the agent that produced the most recent write to this file. MUST be one of the agents listed as a writer in `owner_writes`. Paired with `last_updated_at`, this is the audit trail for the deterministic write order.

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time of the most recent write. Used by Ace's landing-page loader for staleness detection and by regression tooling to diff metadata across reruns (DATA-R1).

indicator.description: Optional. Canonical indicator name (e.g. `hy_ig_spread`) — matches the primary signal column in the master parquet.

expected_direction.description: Optional. Ray-owned. Ex-ante direction hypothesis from the research brief.

observed_direction.description: Optional. Evan-owned. Empirical direction observed in estimation; cross-checked against `expected_direction`.

direction_consistent.description: Optional. Evan-owned. `true` when observed matches expected; `false` triggers a RES-B4 contradiction note.

mechanism.description: Optional. Ray-owned. One-to-two-sentence economic mechanism describing why the indicator relates to the target.

confidence.description: Optional. Evan-owned. Confidence assessment based on method-coverage consistency, sample size, and diagnostics.

key_finding.description: Optional. Evan-owned. One-sentence headline summary of the strongest quantitative finding.

caveats.description: Optional. Ray-owned. List of sample-period, methodology, or interpretation caveats for downstream readers.

narrative_summary.description: Optional. Ray-owned. Short prose summary of the pair's story, rendered by Ace on landing cards and page headers.

related_pair_ids.description: Optional. Dana-owned. Cross-references to other pair_ids that share an indicator family (e.g. variants) or a target (e.g. all SPY pairs). Enables the landing-page 'related pairs' filter.

known_stress_episodes.description: Optional. Dana-owned. Dated stress episodes used for reference-value verification (cross-ref DATA-D7 proposal).

data_provenance.description: Optional. Dana-owned. Source lineage record for the primary indicator series.
